label: Zoho Books
description: Connect Zoho Books to read and write accounting data including invoices, expenses, and contacts.

form.title: Connect Zoho Books
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to access your Zoho Books organization via OAuth 2.0.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (Bring Your Own Client)
methods.description: Create a self-client app in the Zoho API Console, paste your Client ID and Client Secret, and Anton handles the OAuth browser flow. This is the only supported auth path for Zoho Books.
methods.how_to:
## Create a Zoho API Client

Zoho Books uses OAuth 2.0. You need to register your own app in the Zoho API Console to get a Client ID and Client Secret.

### Steps

1. Go to [Zoho API Console](https://api-console.zoho.com/) and sign in with the Zoho account linked to your Books organization.
2. Click **Add Client** and choose **Self Client** (recommended for desktop/server use).
3. Fill in any name (e.g. `Anton`) and click **Create**.
4. On the client's **Client Secret** tab, copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** — paste them into the fields below.
5. Select the **Data Center / Region** that matches where your Zoho account is hosted (e.g. `.com` for US, `.eu` for Europe).
6. Click **Submit**. Anton will open a browser window to Zoho's consent screen.
7. Grant the requested permissions and you'll be redirected back to Anton automatically.

### Notes

- The region you pick here **must match** the Zoho data center where your account lives. If you sign in at `accounts.zoho.eu`, pick **EU**.
- Zoho refresh tokens are long-lived but can be revoked from **API Console → Your App → Revoke Token**.
- The scope `ZohoBooks.fullaccess.all` grants full read/write access to your Books organization. Use `ZohoBooks.contacts.READ` etc. if you need narrower access.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Client ID
fields.description: From Zoho API Console → your Self Client → Client Secret tab.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID; found on the same tab in the API Console.

fields.name: region
fields.label: Data Center / Region
fields.description: Must match the Zoho data center where your account is hosted. Check the domain you use to log in to Zoho (e.g. accounts.zoho.eu → EU).

fields.name: organization_id
fields.label: Organization ID
fields.description: Optional. Your Zoho Books Organization ID (found in Settings → Organization Profile). Required if your account manages multiple organizations.
